column2: Deliver Community Engagement Interventions on protection, prevention of GBV and other forms of violence.
Comic Book sessions for adolescent girls and young women
Local partners effectively respond to GBV and protection risks among new arrivals appropriate to their age, gender, and disability.

Activity2.2: Provide GBV/CP (including Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (SEA)) survivors with comprehensive, age-appropriate, timely and quality GBV/CP/protection case management, including MHPSS, and/or referral services at the holding centres, transit centres and refugee settlements.
GBV Case Management/ Documantation & provision of multisectoral support to GBV survivors
Psychosocial Support/ GBV screening and registration of survivors at the reception and transit centres
Refer individual protection assistance to access survivor fund from UCC
GBV case conferencing: Facilitate Bi annual meetings case conferencing at settlement and district levels (Meetings)
Refer GBV survivors to access in kind material support - dignity kits, hygiene kits from service providers
Update the referral pathways together with other stakeholders. (Pathways)
Activity 2.3 : Establish or maintain/enhance existing Women and Girls Safe Spaces (WGSS) in refugee settlements.
Establish and maintain women and girls safe spaces.
Operationalize the WGSS - Provide integrated services to women and girls at the safe spaces
GBV information counselling to women and girls and provide skilling services to vulnerable women & girls)
Comic Book sessions for adolescent girls and young women
Provide in kind material support to GBV survivors (dignity kits) -(through referrals)
Provide working tools to GBV community structures (touches, gumboots, raincoats, bags, stationery etc)
Training WGSS CMCS on leadership and safe space management and income generating activities [IGA]
Activity 2.4: Strengthen the capacities of key local service providers and community support structures in effective case management and support
Strengthen capacity of key community structures and leaders to support GBV prevention and responses. GBV referral, reporting training and mentorship.
Community members reached with GBV prevention messages by stregthened GBV community structures
Clinical care training for sexual assault survivors
Training for police on existing GBV referral protocols and case conferencing (60% women)
